economic_notes: Nation holds fishing quotas (crab, lobster, shrimp) granted by DFO at no monetary consideration with carrying value of $4,681,000. Multiple fishing vessels operated (Calypso Gespeg I, Gespeg III, Gespeg V, Gespeg VI, Francis Gespeg, Sol-Del-Mar, Anticosti). Forestry operations including maple syrup project and industrial firewood processor. Tourism and cultural site operations. Consolidated subsidiary: Organisme de développement économique et communautaire Mic-Mac de Gaspé inc. (100%). Investment in 50 Class A shares of Salaweg inc.
notes: Qualified audit opinion due to fishing quotas ($4,681,000) recognized as intangible asset on balance sheet, which is non-compliant with Canadian Public Sector Accounting Standards (PSAS require intangibles to be expensed). If compliant, assets and accumulated surplus would be reduced by $4,681,000. Prior year also had a qualified opinion for the same reason. No remuneration schedule found in the provided text. Fiscal year end is March 31, 2021. The statement includes a prior year restatement/adjustment of $1,486,576 in accumulated surplus. Total government transfers calculated as: ISC $2,001,717 + DEC $115,974 + SAA operating $230,077 + CRHPNQ $180,927 + SAA capital $210,712 + Autres subventions $867,085 = $3,606,492; however Autres subventions may include non-government sources so osr adjusted accordingly. Trust fund managed by ISC not included in financial statements (balance $23,423). Debt service shown is principal repayment only; interest was $115,696.
